_comment: The exact sherpa-onnx speaker-diarization sidecar + default models ANVIL ships, keyed by
target. Single source of truth for scripts/fetch-sherpa.ps1 (windows-x86_64) and
scripts/fetch-sherpa-macos.sh (macos-aarch64 / macos-x86_64). Mirrors the whisper pin's
targets-map shape; the target string is format!("{}-{}", consts::OS, consts::ARCH).
sherpa-onnx is run as a SEPARATE PROCESS (sherpa-onnx-offline-speaker-diarization) and is
never linked into ANVIL. The engine binary is Apache-2.0 (k2-fsa/sherpa-onnx); the bundled
onnxruntime is MIT (Microsoft ONNX Runtime), redistributed by the sherpa release. Both are
cleanly redistributable in an MIT app. The 'shared' build is used because it carries the
runnable CLIs + onnxruntime; the diarization exe STATICALLY links the sherpa C++ core, so
only onnxruntime is needed beside it - proven per-target by otool -L / dumpbin.
WINDOWS (windows-x86_64): the win-x64-shared build. Needs onnxruntime.dll + its
providers-shared stub next to the exe (flat layout).
MACOS (macos-aarch64 + macos-x86_64): ONE universal2 (fat arm64+x86_64) artifact, staged
byte-identically into BOTH target dirs (same hashes) for bundle symmetry. otool -L on the
mac binary shows it needs ONLY libonnxruntime.<ver>.dylib - there is NO providers-shared
equivalent on macOS (single dylib). The binary's baked rpath is @loader_path/../lib, so the
bin/ + lib/ sibling structure is PRESERVED (not flattened) and runs env-free with no fixup.
The upstream mac binary is adhoc linker-signed (runs unquarantined; the release-signing lane
re-signs every Mach-O with a Developer ID + hardened runtime for notarization).
MODELS. Diarization needs a segmentation model + a speaker-embedding model to work at all,
so - unlike whisper weights (which the Models screen downloads on demand) - the two DEFAULT
models are bundled so diarization works out of the box. They are OS-INDEPENDENT (reused
across every target), so they stay a top-level models[] array, not per-target. Their sha256s
MUST equal the pins in crates/anvil-asr/src/model.rs KNOWN_DIARIZATION_MODELS; the anvil-asr
test `sherpa_pin_models_match_the_catalog` fails if they ever drift.
- segmentation: pyannote-segmentation-3.0, MIT ((c) 2022 CNRS). Redistributable.
- embedding: NVIDIA NeMo TitaNet-small, CC-BY-4.0 - REDISTRIBUTABLE WITH ATTRIBUTION.
It is the default (not the Apache-2.0 WeSpeaker CAM++) because CAM++ is degenerate on
the shipped Windows onnxruntime (collapses to one speaker); see model.rs. The required
attribution ships as scripts/licenses/titanet-small-ATTRIBUTION.txt.
Like whisper, anvil-asr's DiarizeSidecar::locate() does not hash-check at run time; these
pins are the provisioning-time integrity gate enforced by the fetch scripts.
sha256 = RAW file hash (provisioning gate). content_sha256 (macOS members: the diarization exe
+ onnxruntime dylib) = the signing-INDEPENDENT Mach-O content hash used for a macOS
bundle-vs-pin check (the release lane Developer-ID re-signs both). NOTE: the upstream
universal2 x86_64 slice is UNSIGNED, but codesign signs it at packaging time, so content_sha256
is recorded from a FULLY (ad-hoc) signed copy = what the shipped, Developer-ID-signed .app
hashes to. See crates/anvil-asr/src/pin.rs macho_content_sha256 + ADR-012.

macos-aarch64.note: universal2 single artifact; identical bytes/hashes to macos-x86_64. rpath @loader_path/../lib, no fixup.

macos-x86_64.note: same universal2 artifact as macos-aarch64 (identical bytes/hashes); staged separately for per-target bundle symmetry.
